name: DURHAM, JOHNSTONE SCHOOL RH8

surface_od_m.qualifier: absent — no AOD/Ground Level datum given; depths measured from G.L.

units: metres below ground level, transcribed verbatim from a typed summary strata log — depth = base of interval below ground

annotations: 'Coal Traces' = coal fragments within the glacial sand, not an in-situ seam; the borehole bottoms in drift. Adjacent RH9 on the same sheet logs a thin in-situ 'Coal' at 12.20-12.30 m.

notes: Page 1 = typed summary strata log (Durham Johnston School, Whinney Hill, Durham, Co. Durham; job 950197) covering three light-cable-percussion / rotary-open holes RH2, RH8, RH9; only RH8 transcribed here. Page 2 = HJT Solmek borehole location plan (dwg 950197/L1, dated FEB 96) with no strata — used only to confirm hole positions. RH8 is a shallow all-drift hole: boulder clay over glacial sand, bottoming at 30.00 m; no bedrock, no seatearth, no named coal seam reached. No AOD datum on the sheet, so surface_od_m is absent. No pencil marginalia or water observations on this typed record. Depths run continuously (1.30 / 10.45 / 30.00) with no arithmetic slips. Sheet header spells it 'JOHNSTON' (no final e); the record name 'JOHNSTONE' is retained per the task's hole identity.
